label: Parse JSON
description: Parse a JSON string into a structured object. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for the common 'LLM returned a JSON string' case.

settings.name: input
settings.label: Input (template or upstream)
settings.description: Path-template to the JSON string. Omit to use the upstream connection's input directly. If the input is already an object, it passes through unchanged.

settings.name: onError
settings.label: On parse error
settings.description: fail = throw and stop the run; null = return { parsed: null }; empty_object = return { parsed: {} }. The parseError string is always populated on failure for downstream branches.

ai.shortDescription: Parse a JSON string into an object with explicit error handling.
ai.whenToUse: When an upstream node hands you a JSON string (LLM output, HTTP body, MCP tool content) and you need the structured value before downstream consumes it.
ai.examplePrompt: parse the LLM response body as JSON, fall back to empty object on error
ai.promptHints: Output: {{steps.X.parsed}} (object/array/scalar) + {{steps.X.parseError}} (null on success). Already-parsed inputs pass through unchanged.
